subject: Accounting
title: Accrued Expenses
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Accrued Expenses.

hook.text: Employees worked all December but won't get paid until January 5th. December still owes them.

buildup.text: Accrued expenses are costs incurred but not yet paid—wages, interest, utilities.

discovery.text: An adjusting entry records the expense in December and creates a liability for the amount owed.

twist.text: Skip this and December's expenses look too low—inflating profit that isn't real.

climax.text: Accruals ensure that expenses appear in the period they belong to, not when cash finally moves.

punchline.text: You owe it now. Record it now. Pay it later.

quiz.question: What is an accrued expense?
options: A cost incurred but not yet paid
A cost that has been refunded
